name: Polynesian Cultural Center
tagline: Six island villages, canoe rides, fire-knife dancing, and a luau — the best cultural experience on Oahu for families.
bestTime: Arrive at opening to see all six villages before the evening show; weekdays for smaller crowds
kamaaina: Kamaaina annual pass and discounted packages available with Hawaii ID
talkStory: Founded in 1963, the center was originally staffed by BYU-Hawaii students who performed and demonstrated crafts to pay tuition -- a tradition continuing today with over 100 student employees from Pacific Island nations. Six villages represent Hawaii, Samoa, Tonga, Fiji, Tahiti, and Aotearoa. The evening 'Ha: Breath of Life' show features a 100-person cast with the world-famous Samoan fire-knife dance. Arrive at 12:30 sharp to start with the least-crowded village.
